warning_message.title: Development environment
warning_message.content: This is a development environment for testing purposes. Production endpoints will be configured during deployment.

ui.playground_note: The browser runs the real request. The server must allow CORS to respond from the browser.
ui.playground_error_status: We could not connect. Check your connection and try again.
ui.playground_error_body: The request could not be completed. Try again in a moment.
ui.search_empty_hint: Check the spelling or try another term.
ui.not_found_title: We could not find this page
ui.not_found_desc: The link may have changed or no longer exists. Start from the home page or search for what you need.
ui.error_hint: Technical details were logged to the console.

api.name: capidocs API
api.description: Documentation platform API for technical content management. Provides content operations, user management, and real-time collaboration features.

features.title: Core Features
features.subtitle: Everything you need for technical documentation

items.title: Content Management
items.description: Create, edit, and organize technical documentation with version control.

items.title: Team Collaboration
items.description: Multi-user editing with permissions, comments, and review workflows.

items.title: Secure Access
items.description: Role-based access control with API keys and authentication tokens.

items.title: Real-time Sync
items.description: Live updates and notifications for document changes and comments.

items.title: Advanced Search
items.description: Full-text search across all documentation with filtering and faceting.

items.title: Developer Friendly
items.description: API-first design with comprehensive REST endpoints and SDKs.

steps.title: Quick Integration
steps.subtitle: Get started in three simple steps

items.title: Get API Key
items.description: Sign up and obtain your API credentials from the developer dashboard.

items.title: Make First Call
items.description: Test your integration with our sandbox environment and sample code.

items.title: Go Live
items.description: Deploy to production with confidence using our monitoring tools.

quick_start.title: Quick Start
quick_start.subtitle: Try the API with this example request

content.title: Getting Started
content.description: Learn how to integrate our API into your application quickly and efficiently.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: Our REST API provides a comprehensive set of endpoints for building modern web applications. The API follows RESTful principles and uses standard HTTP methods and status codes for consistent interaction patterns.

sections.title: Base URL
sections.content: All API requests should be made to our base URL with the appropriate endpoint path:

sections.title: Request Format
sections.content: The API accepts and returns JSON data. Always include the Content-Type header in your requests:

sections.title: Response Format
sections.content: All responses follow a consistent structure with success indicators and appropriate HTTP status codes:

content.title: Authentication
content.description: Secure your API requests using our authentication methods. We support multiple authentication schemes for different use cases.

sections.title: API Key Authentication
sections.content: The simplest authentication method using API keys. Include your API key in the Authorization header with Bearer scheme.

sections.title: OAuth 2.0
sections.content: For applications that need to access user data on behalf of users, we support OAuth 2.0 with authorization code flow.
list: Redirect users to our authorization endpoint
Receive authorization code after user consent
Exchange code for access token
Use access token for API requests

sections.title: JWT Tokens
sections.content: JSON Web Tokens provide a stateless authentication method ideal for microservices and distributed systems.
sections.note: JWT tokens expire after 24 hours. Implement token refresh logic in your application.

sections.title: Rate Limiting
sections.content: API requests are rate limited to ensure fair usage and system stability:

content.title: API Endpoints
content.description: Complete reference for all available API endpoints with request/response examples.

sections.title: User Management
sections.content: Endpoints for managing user accounts, profiles, and authentication.

sections.title: Data Operations
sections.content: CRUD operations for your application data with advanced querying capabilities.

content.title: Webhooks
content.description: Configure real-time notifications for events in your application using webhooks.

sections.title: Webhook Events
sections.content: Available webhook events that you can subscribe to:
list: user.created - New user registration
user.updated - User profile changes
data.created - New data item created
data.updated - Data item modified
system.error - System error occurred

sections.title: Webhook Configuration
sections.content: Configure webhook endpoints in your developer dashboard or via API:

sections.title: Webhook Payload
sections.content: Example webhook payload structure:

content.title: Error Handling
content.description: Understanding API error responses and how to handle them in your application.

sections.title: HTTP Status Codes
sections.content: Standard HTTP status codes used by the API:

sections.title: Error Response Format
sections.content: All error responses follow a consistent structure:

sections.title: Common Error Codes
sections.content: Application-specific error codes you may encounter:

rows: API key is invalid or expired

content.title: Components
content.description: Building blocks for clear, pleasant documentation. Every component is declared in JSON inside a section; no HTML is written.

sections.title: What a section can hold
sections.content: Content pages are made of sections. Each section accepts these fields and can combine several; capidocs renders them in a fixed, accessible order.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Section heading. Appears in the table of contents.

fields.name: content
fields.description: Plain-text paragraph. Always escaped: HTML is not allowed.

fields.name: callout
fields.description: Highlighted notice with type, title and content.

fields.name: cards
fields.description: Card grid with icon, title, description and link.

fields.name: tabs
fields.description: Tabs with alternative content (text or code).

fields.name: accordion
fields.description: Collapsible question-and-answer items.

fields.name: code
fields.description: Code block with language and optional title.

fields.name: code_group
fields.description: Group of code blocks with per-language tabs.

fields.name: fields
fields.description: Parameter list with type, required, default and description.

fields.name: table
fields.description: Table with headers and rows.

fields.name: list
fields.description: Simple bullet list.

fields.name: steps
fields.description: Numbered steps with title, description and optional link.

fields.name: note
fields.description: Informative note in a subtle box.

fields.name: warning
fields.description: Warning in a box with an alert border.

sections.title: Callouts
sections.content: They highlight important information without breaking the reading flow. There are five types: note, info, tip, warning and danger (success and check are aliases of tip, error of danger). This is how the one below is declared:

code.title: Section with a callout

callout.title: Tip
callout.content: Use callouts to draw attention to key steps or warnings without breaking the reading flow.

sections.title: Warning callout
sections.content: The warning type uses amber and an alert icon.

callout.title: Heads up
callout.content: This endpoint is rate limited. Cache responses where possible.

sections.title: Danger callout
sections.content: The danger type is reserved for destructive or irreversible actions.

callout.title: Irreversible
callout.content: Deleting an API key revokes access immediately and cannot be undone.

sections.title: Cards
sections.content: They group links or highlights into a responsive grid. Each card accepts icon (a Font Awesome class), title, description and link.

code.title: Cards declaration

cards.title: Quickstart
cards.description: Get up and running in under five minutes.

cards.title: Authentication
cards.description: Secure your requests with API keys.

sections.title: Tabs
sections.content: They show alternative content in the same space. Each tab accepts label and then content or code.

code.title: Tabs declaration

tabs.label: Overview
tabs.content: Tabs let readers switch between related content without leaving the page.

code.content:
git clone https://github.com/your-user/capidocs.git
cd capidocs && python3 -m http.server 8080

sections.title: Accordions
sections.content: They tuck away supplementary details until the reader needs them. Ideal for frequently asked questions.

accordion.title: How is rate limiting applied?
accordion.content: Requests are limited per API key. Limits vary by plan and reset every hour.

accordion.title: Is there pagination?
accordion.content: Yes. Use the page and limit query parameters on any list endpoint.

sections.title: Code groups
sections.content: They present the same operation in several languages with switchable tabs. The full declaration lives on the Code blocks page.

sections.title: Parameter fields
sections.content: They document parameters with name, type, requirement, default value and description. It is the same visual format endpoint pages use.

fields.name: page
fields.description: Page number for pagination.

fields.name: limit
fields.description: Items per page (maximum 100).

fields.name: query
fields.description: Search term to filter results.

sections.title: Tables
sections.content: For simple tabular data: headers defines the columns and rows the rows.

sections.title: Lists, notes and warnings
sections.content: Lists are arrays of strings; note and warning are text boxes with different emphasis.
sections.note: This is an informative note: useful for clarifications that do not warrant a callout.
sections.warning: This is a warning: the text is shown in a box with an alert border.
list: Items are declared as strings inside an array.
They render as bullets with proper spacing.
All text is escaped: HTML is not interpreted.

sections.title: Steps
sections.content: They guide the reader through an ordered process. Each step accepts number, title, description and an optional link.

steps.title: Clone the repository
steps.description: Download capidocs and enter the project directory.

steps.title: Edit the configuration
steps.description: Describe your API and your pages in config/api-spec.json.

steps.title: Publish
steps.description: Upload the files to any static hosting. There is no build step.

content.title: Configuration
content.description: All of capidocs is driven by a single file: config/api-spec.json. No build, no dependencies.

sections.title: A single file
sections.content: The config/api-spec.json file defines the brand, the navigation, the pages and the endpoints. The site loads it with one fetch at startup: edit, reload and see the change.

code.title: Overall structure

sections.note: Changes apply on page reload. There is no mandatory build step.

sections.title: Brand and logos
sections.content: With use_logos enabled, the navbar uses the logo matching the theme and drops the text name and badge. When disabled, brand_icon is shown next to the name.

sections.title: Theme
sections.content: default_theme accepts system, light or dark. With system, the site follows the operating system preference and reacts live if it changes. When the reader picks a theme with the navbar button, their choice is saved and takes priority.

callout.title: Light and dark parity
callout.content: Every component, including code blocks, has its own palette in both themes. No dark boxes on the light theme.

sections.title: Navigation bar
sections.content: tabs creates top-level tabs in the center of the navbar; each one shows only the sidebar groups listed in groups (the navigation group is called nav_guides and the rest use their section title). links adds links on the right and cta paints a highlighted button.
sections.note: With fewer than two tabs the tab bar is not shown and the sidebar presents every group.

sections.title: Table of contents
sections.content: The right column is generated automatically from the page headings. You can disable it, require a minimum number of headings or exclude specific pages and texts.
sections.note: Endpoint pages do not show a TOC: that column is used by the request and response example panels.

sections.title: Page titles
sections.content: page_titles controls the document title per route, with a base title and a separator.

sections.title: Versions
sections.content: versions publishes several specifications at once. The selector appears next to the logo; switching versions loads the matching spec and the choice is remembered in the browser.

sections.title: Interface texts
sections.content: lang sets the document language and site_config.ui lets you translate or customize any fixed interface text. When a key is not defined, the default Spanish text is used. To publish the site in several languages, declare site_config.languages: one spec per language and a selector appears in the navbar.

fields.name: nav_guides
fields.description: Title of the main sidebar navigation group.

fields.name: try_it
fields.description: Text of the button that opens the playground on endpoint pages.

fields.name: authorizations_title
fields.description: Heading of the authorization section.

fields.name: query_params_title
fields.description: Heading of the query parameters.

fields.name: body_title
fields.description: Heading of the request body.

fields.name: responses_title
fields.description: Heading of the response list.

fields.name: required
fields.description: Label of the required-field chip.

fields.name: example
fields.description: Prefix of each field example line.

fields.name: copy_code
fields.description: Accessible label of the copy button on code panels.

fields.name: feedback_q
fields.description: Feedback question at the bottom of every page.

fields.name: copy_link
fields.description: Text of the copy-link button.

fields.name: prev
fields.description: Label of the previous-page link.

fields.name: next
fields.description: Label of the next-page link.

content.title: Code blocks
content.description: Syntax highlighting with its own theme in light and dark, a header with title and a copy button on every block.

sections.title: Basic block
sections.content: A block is declared with language, content and an optional title. Without a title, the header shows the language name; untitled plain-text blocks render without a header.

code.title: Example with a title

sections.title: How it is declared
sections.content: Inside any section, the code field accepts this object:
sections.note: On endpoints, long examples can live in real files under config/examples and be referenced with the file field: you edit them with highlighting in your editor and they load on the fly.

sections.title: Languages
sections.content: Highlighting uses Prism with automatic grammar loading: any language supported by Prism works just by declaring it. Some common ones:
list: bash and shell for the terminal
javascript, typescript and json
python, php, ruby, go and java
yaml, html and css

sections.title: Code groups
sections.content: code_group shows the same operation in several languages with tabs. Each item carries label, language and content.

code_group.label: cURL
code_group.content:
curl https://api.capidocs.dev/v2/data \
  -X POST \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY' \
  -d '{"title": "Nuevo elemento"}'

code_group.label: JavaScript
Python
code_group.content:
import requests
requests.post('https://api.capidocs.dev/v2/data',
    headers={'Authorization': 'Bearer YOUR_API_KEY'},
    json={'title': 'Nuevo elemento'})

sections.title: Copy to clipboard
sections.content: Every block includes a copy button: in the header when the block has a title and floating when it does not. Endpoint example panels always copy the active tab.

callout.title: Zero configuration
callout.content: The copy button is added automatically to every new block, even content generated at runtime.

sections.title: Customizing the palette
sections.content: Syntax colors live in CSS variables with independent values for light and dark themes. To match your brand, just redefine them.

content.title: Document your API
content.description: Turn your specification into an interactive reference: two columns, per-language examples, responses by status code and a try-it console.

sections.title: Anatomy of an endpoint
sections.content: Each endpoint is defined in the endpoints object with its method, path and request and response details. With that, capidocs paints the whole page: endpoint bar, parameters as field rows and example panels pinned on the right.

code.title: endpoints.create-item

sections.note: Segments in braces, such as {collectionId}, are highlighted as path parameters in the endpoint bar.

sections.title: Showing up in the sidebar
sections.content: The endpoint is registered in a sidebar section with the same id. The method renders as a colored chip: green for GET, blue for POST, amber for PUT, red for DELETE and violet for PATCH.

sections.title: Parameters and body
sections.content: Headers, query parameters and body properties are documented once and rendered as field rows with type, origin and requirement chips, plus an example line. The schema required array marks which properties carry the red chip.

code.title: request

sections.title: Responses
sections.content: Each status code carries a description and, optionally, examples with real values. On the page they are listed as rows with a colored chip and they feed the right panel: one tab per status with its example JSON.

sections.title: Per-language examples
sections.content: code_examples defines the tabs of the request panel. Code can be inline or in a real file under config/examples, edited with highlighting in your editor and loaded on the fly. If you define none, capidocs generates the cURL command automatically from the endpoint.

sections.title: Playground
sections.content: The Try it button on the endpoint bar unfolds a console with headers, parameters and body preloaded with the examples. The request actually runs from the browser and shows the status, timing and response body.

callout.title: CORS
callout.content: Since the request leaves the reader's browser, your API must allow CORS from the documentation domain for the console to receive the response.

sections.title: Importing from OpenAPI
sections.content: If you already have an OpenAPI 3.x specification in JSON, the bundled converter generates the full api-spec.json: endpoints, parameters, schemas, response examples and cURL commands.
sections.note: The converter has no dependencies. If your specification is in YAML, convert it to JSON first.

content.title: Platform features
content.description: Search, versions, page actions and AI-ready documentation. All included with zero configuration.

sections.title: Instant search
sections.content: The search opens with the keyboard shortcut or from the navbar and finds pages, sections and endpoints with fuzzy matching. The index is built in the browser from the configuration; if you generated search-index.json with the light build, that precomputed index is used.

callout.title: Shortcut
callout.content: Press cmd+K on macOS or ctrl+K on Windows and Linux to search from any page.

sections.title: Version selector
sections.content: If you publish several versions of your API, the selector next to the logo switches between specifications. The reader's choice is remembered between visits. The configuration is described on the Configuration page.

sections.title: Page actions
sections.content: The three-dot menu next to the breadcrumb offers actions designed for sharing and for working with AI assistants:
list: Copy the page as Markdown
View the raw Markdown of the page
Open the page in Claude with context
Open the page in ChatGPT with context

sections.title: Feedback and navigation
sections.content: Every page closes with a helpfulness question (thumbs up or down), a copy-link button and automatic previous and next navigation following the configuration order.

sections.title: AI-ready documentation
sections.content: The light build generates llms.txt and llms-full.txt at the site root: a structured summary and the full content in plain text, designed so language models can consume your documentation without scraping.

sections.title: Optional light build
sections.content: The site works without compiling anything. If you want the precomputed extras, a single dependency-free Node command generates the search index, the AI files, the sitemap and robots.txt.
sections.note: Run it whenever the content changes if you want the precomputed index and the AI files to stay up to date.

sections.title: Deployment
sections.content: capidocs is a pure static site: HTML, CSS and JavaScript with no server. It deploys to any static hosting, such as Cloudflare Pages, Netlify, GitHub Pages or your own nginx.
sections.note: Navigation uses hash routing, so you do not need redirects or an SPA fallback.

users-endpoint.title: List Users
users-endpoint.description: Retrieve a list of users with optional filtering and pagination. Supports various query parameters for customizing the results.

code_examples.name: JavaScript

data-endpoint.description: Create a new data item in the system. Supports various data types and custom metadata.
